query: SK Hynix ADR Nasdaq listing SKHY first trading date July 2026 latest news, pricing date, roadshow status, SEC review as of late June 2026. Is July 10 still the expected trading date? Any reports of delay or schedule slippage?
answer:
## Bottom line

**Yes, July 10, 2026 is still the officially expected trading date for SK Hynix's Nasdaq ADR debut (ticker SKHY), and there are no reports of delay or schedule slippage** as of the latest reporting (July 1–2, 2026).

Key points:
- **On track / slightly ahead of schedule.** Earlier June 10 Reuters reporting had floated an August debut; the confirmed board-approved plan (June 24) moved the target up to July 10.
- **Most recent milestone:** SK Hynix filed **Amendment No. 1 to its Form F-1** on SEC EDGAR on **June 30, 2026**, finalizing the ticker (SKHY) and venue (Nasdaq Global Select Market), stating it will list "as soon as practicable" after registration effectiveness.
- **Roadshow:** A non-deal roadshow was reportedly completed by mid-June; final pricing follows book-building/demand forecast.
- **SEC review:** Ongoing through late June — no confirmation found that the F-1 was declared effective as of July 2. Korean FSS review was expected to complete around July 3.
- **Only caveat:** SK Hynix consistently labels July 10 as "tentative and subject to change" pending SEC effectiveness, pricing, and market conditions — standard offering language, not evidence of a delay.

**Offering:** up to 17.79M ADSs (~2.5% of shares), targeting ~45.45 trillion won (~$29.4B) — potentially the largest ADR offering ever. Estimated ~$166/ADS. Related dates: subscription/payment July 14, Korean new-share listing July 29.

sources.title: South Korea's biggest chipmaker SK Hynix plans to raise $29 billion via Nasdaq listing as soon as July 10
sources.snippet: SK Hynix said it expects to start trading on July 10, but added that the dates were tentative and subject to change.

sources.title: SK Hynix plans $29 billion Nasdaq ADR listing in 2026
sources.snippet: SK Hynix filed with the SEC on Wednesday to raise approximately $29.65 billion; trading expected to begin July 10, up to 17.79 million new shares at 45.45 trillion won.

sources.snippet: Amended registration statement filed Monday to list under symbol SKHY; up to 17.79 million new ADS (~2.5% of 712.7M shares); shares expected to begin trading July 10.

sources.snippet: Form F-1 for ADS under ticker SKHY; ~$29.4B; ADS projected near $166; HSBC forecasts ~20% valuation lift; underwriters BofA, Citi, Goldman, JPMorgan; trading expected July 10.
